01_initial_request.user_intent: To extend DeepTracer-1.0 functionality by incorporating the identification of nucleic acids along with amino acids for automated and accurate macromolecular modeling
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Cα prediction matching averages of 85-90% and lower root-mean-square deviation (RMSD) values

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cryo-EM maps contain mixed densities of proteins and nucleic acids that need to be separated for accurate modeling. [Gap] Previous DeepTracer-1.0 did not account for map regions involving nucleic acids, leading to misidentification. [Decision] To achieve accurate macromolecular complex modeling, chose to use DeepTracer-2.0 segmentation network (U-Net architecture) to perform density segmentation on cryo-EM maps, expecting to obtain separated amino acid and nucleotide density maps.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Density maps separated into amino acid density (cyan, combining α-helical, β-strand, and unassigned probabilities) and nucleic acid density (dark blue)

references.citation: Mostosi P, Schindelin H, Kollmannsberger P, et al. Haruspex: a neural network for the automatic identification of oligonucleotides and protein secondary structure in Cryo-electron microscopy maps. Angew Chem Int Ed Engl 2020;59:14788–95.
references.relevance: Provides the U-Net architecture foundation for segmentation network design

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Separated density maps need to be normalized and resized before atom position prediction. [Gap] Raw density values vary and maps have variable sizes. [Decision] To achieve consistent input for U-Net prediction, chose to use DeepTracer-2.0 preprocessing pipeline to perform normalization and resizing on separated density maps, expecting to obtain standardized 64³ voxel maps with values normalized to 0-1 range.

parameters.input: separated amino acid and nucleotide density maps

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Preprocessed density maps normalized and resized to 64³ voxel size for both amino acid and nucleotide pipelines

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Amino acid backbone atoms need to be identified from preprocessed protein density maps. [Gap] Cα positions and other backbone atoms must be predicted accurately for protein structure modeling. [Decision] To achieve accurate amino acid structure prediction, chose to use Amino Acid U-Net to perform atom position prediction on preprocessed amino acid density maps, expecting to obtain Cα and backbone atom positions.

parameters.training_data: 1800 experimental maps and corresponding deposited model structures

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cα and backbone atom positions determined for amino acid structures

references.citation: Pfab J, Phan NM, Si D. DeepTracer for fast de novo cryo-EM protein structure modeling and special studies on CoV-related complexes. PNAS 2021;118:1–4
references.relevance: Describes the original amino acid U-Net architecture from DeepTracer-1.0

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Nucleotide critical atoms need to be identified from preprocessed nucleic acid density maps. [Gap] Phosphate and carbon atom positions must be predicted for nucleic acid structure modeling. [Decision] To achieve accurate nucleotide structure prediction, chose to use Nucleotide U-Net to perform atom position prediction on preprocessed nucleotide density maps, expecting to obtain phosphate (P), C1', and C4' atom positions.

parameters.output_channels: 4 channels (phosphate atom P, carbon one atom C1', carbon four atom C4', no atom)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Phosphate and carbon atom positions determined for nucleotide structures

references.citation: Lawson CL, Patwardhan A, Berman HM, et al. EMDataBank unified data resource for 3DEM. Nucleic Acids Res 2016;44:D396–403.
references.relevance: Source of EMDB/PDB pairs used for nucleotide network training

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Nucleotide backbone structure needs to be determined from density maps. [Gap] Voxels must be classified as sugar-phosphate backbone, nitrogenous base, or neither. [Decision] To achieve accurate nucleotide backbone modeling, chose to use Backbone U-Net to perform backbone classification on preprocessed nucleotide density maps, expecting to obtain backbone structure classification.

parameters.output_channels: 3 channels (sugar phosphate backbone, nitrogenous base, not in either group)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Voxels classified as sugar-phosphate backbone, nitrogenous base, or neither

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Predicted phosphate and carbon atoms need to be connected into biologically consistent nucleotide chains. [Gap] Raw U-Net predictions may contain false positives and need refinement. [Decision] To achieve accurate nucleotide chain modeling, chose to use Brickworx to perform nucleotide postprocessing on predicted atom positions, expecting to obtain refined sugar-phosphate backbone structure.

parameters.sugar_pucker: C3' endo (A-DNA or RNA) or C2' endo (B-DNA)
parameters.pseudotorsion_method: angles between C1' atoms and P

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Refined phosphate and carbon atom positions with sugar-phosphate backbone consistent with DNA/RNA biological principles

references.citation: Chojnowski G, Walen´ T, Piątkowski P, et al. Brickworx builds recurrent RNA and DNA structural motifs into medium- and low-resolution electron-density maps. Acta Cryst D 2015;71: 697–705.
references.relevance: Describes the Brickworx modeling system used for nucleotide postprocessing

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Predicted amino acid backbone atoms need to be connected into polypeptide chains. [Gap] Atoms must be connected correctly respecting input sequence. [Decision] To achieve accurate amino acid chain modeling, chose to use DeepTracer-2.0 amino acid postprocessing to perform chain connection on predicted Cα positions, expecting to obtain complete polypeptide chains.

parameters.matching_threshold: Cα position within 3 Å of model
parameters.input: predicted Cα and backbone atom positions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Connected amino acid backbone atoms forming complete polypeptide chains

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Separate protein and DNA/RNA structures have been modeled independently. [Gap] Final macromolecular complex model needs to combine both structures. [Decision] To achieve complete macromolecular complex modeling, chose to use DeepTracer-2.0 pipeline to perform model combination on protein and nucleotide structures, expecting to obtain final protein-DNA/RNA complex model.

parameters.input: protein structure and DNA/RNA structure predictions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Complete macromolecular structure combining amino acid and nucleotide components

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] DeepTracer-2.0 predictions need to be evaluated against ground truth and compared with existing methods. [Gap] Performance metrics must be quantified to validate the method. [Decision] To achieve method validation, chose to use Phenix map_to_model as comparison baseline to perform evaluation on 20 experimental cryo-EM maps, expecting to obtain quantitative performance metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Comparison metrics obtained for RMSD, % matching, % sequence matching, % false positives, phosphate precision, and nucleotide precision

references.citation: Liebschner D, Afonine PV, Baker ML, et al. Macromolecular structure determination using X-rays, neutrons and electrons: recent developments in Phenix. Acta Cryst D 2019;75:861–77.
references.relevance: Describes the Phenix software suite used for comparison

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Quantitative comparison with deposited model structures and Phenix pipeline on 20 experimental protein-RNA/DNA cryo-EM maps

Cα_RMSD.interpretation: DeepTracer shows lower RMSD values for Cα positions compared to Phenix, indicating better accuracy

Cα_matching_percentage.interpretation: Achieves the target Cα prediction matching averages of 85-90% as stated in research objective

sequence_matching_percentage.interpretation: DeepTracer shows better sequence matching than Phenix for amino acid type prediction

false_positive_percentage.interpretation: DeepTracer produces fewer false positive predictions compared to Phenix

phosphate_precision.interpretation: Phosphate atom positions within 1.5 Å of reference structure with high precision

nucleotide_precision.interpretation: Both P and C1' atom positions within 1.5 Å and 1.0 Å respectively with high precision

runtime.value: 5-6 minutes vs 6 hours to 1 day for Phenix
runtime.interpretation: DeepTracer pipeline is exponentially faster than Phenix pipeline

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved. DeepTracer-2.0 successfully extends DeepTracer-1.0 functionality by incorporating nucleic acid identification along with amino acids. Cα prediction matching averages of 85-90% were achieved with lower RMSD values compared to Phenix. The pipeline demonstrates improved accuracy and efficiency for automated macromolecular complex modeling from cryo-EM maps, though RNA and single-stranded nucleotide predictions show room for improvement with more high-resolution training data.
